creator: emz@norma.perm.ru
text:
Same thing on 11.0-STABLE r315957.
12 panics in a row on during ~ one month.

I have crashdumps in case someone needs them.

Typical bt:

savecore: reboot after panic:                                                                                                               Solaris(panic): zfs: allocating allocated segment(offset=964258332672 size=16384)
Nov 30 03:58:03 san savecore: reboot after panic:                                                                                                               Solaris(panic): zfs: allocating allocated segment(offset=964258332672 size=16384)
savecore: writing core to /var/crash/vmcore.9
Writing crash summary to /var/crash/core.txt.9.
Clearing /tmp (X related).
Starting ctld.
NFS on reserved port only=YES
NFSv4 is disabled
Starting rpcbind.
/etc/rc.d/mountd: WARNING: /etc/exports is not readable.
Starting mountd.
Starting nfsd.
Starting statd.
Starting lockd.
Processing uwsgi profile: api
Starting uwsgi.
[uWSGI] getting INI configuration from /usr/local/etc/uwsgi_api.ini
Processing uwsgi profile: zfsreplica
Starting uwsgi.
[uWSGI] getting INI configuration from /usr/local/etc/uwsgi_zfsreplica.ini
Updating motd:.
Mounting late filesystems:.
Starting ntpd.
Performing sanity check on nginx configuration:
Nov 30 03:58:32 san ntpd[1083]: restrict: ignoring line 21, address/host 'fd00::/16' unusable.
nginx: the configuration file /usr/local/etc/nginx/nginx.conf syntax is ok
nginx: configuration file /usr/local/etc/nginx/nginx.conf test is successful
Starting nginx.
Starting bird.
Configuring vt: blanktime.
Performing sanity check on sshd configuration.
Starting sshd.
Starting sendmail_submit.
Starting sendmail_msp_queue.
Starting cron.
Starting background file system checks in 60 seconds.

Thu Nov 30 03:58:33 +05 2017
Nov 30 04:51:35 san ctld[2278]: 192.168.1.72 (iqn.2016-04.net.playkey:test-kvm): read: Connection reset by peer
Nov 30 11:07:02 san ctld[2537]: 192.168.1.72 (iqn.2016-04.net.playkey:test-kvm): read: Connection reset by peer
WARNING: 192.168.1.57 (iqn.2010-04.org.ipxe:03000200-0400-0500-0006-000700080009): no ping reply (NOP-Out) after 5 seconds; dropping connection
WARNING: 192.168.1.57 (iqn.2010-04.org.ipxe:03000200-0400-0500-0006-000700080009): no ping reply (NOP-Out) after 5 seconds; dropping connection
Nov 30 17:29:48 san su: failed to get password: authentication error
Nov 30 17:29:50 san su: emz to root on /dev/pts/1
WARNING: 192.168.1.57 (iqn.2010-04.org.ipxe:03000200-0400-0500-0006-000700080009): no ping reply (NOP-Out) after 5 seconds; dropping connection
panic: Solaris(panic): zfs: allocating allocated segment(offset=1102832721920 size=16384)

cpuid = 4
KDB: stack backtrace:
#0 0xffffffff80a8c7c7 at kdb_backtrace+0x67
#1 0xffffffff80a49ae6 at vpanic+0x186
#2 0xffffffff80a49953 at panic+0x43
#3 0xffffffff82432192 at vcmn_err+0xc2
#4 0xffffffff821a4b1a at zfs_panic_recover+0x5a
#5 0xffffffff8218e035 at range_tree_add+0x235
#6 0xffffffff8218cb86 at metaslab_free_dva+0x276
#7 0xffffffff8218cc31 at metaslab_free+0x81
#8 0xffffffff821cfbda at zio_dva_free+0x1a
#9 0xffffffff821ccb8d at zio_execute+0x23d
#10 0xffffffff80a9d447 at taskqueue_run_locked+0x117
#11 0xffffffff80a9e548 at taskqueue_thread_loop+0xc8
#12 0xffffffff80a0dd05 at fork_exit+0x85
#13 0xffffffff80e92f6e at fork_trampoline+0xe
Uptime: 13h42m11s
Dumping 2638 out of 24531 MB:..1%..11%..21%..31%..41%..51%..61%..71%..81%..91%

This is also related to zfs corruption on the pool, which happens after these panics. May be this is somehow related to the fact that this host runs iSCSI target, probably panics occur when working with specific data which iSCSI target serves.
